title: docs(perf): reclassify audit finding N1 (art-jobs store) as refuted
intent: Corrects PERFORMANCE_AUDIT_2026-07-04.md: finding N1 (JSON art-jobs store, 22ms/parse) was over-classified as a confirmed bottleneck (#9). The store is dormant — 43 records, all created in a 32-day window (Jan-Feb 2026), no writes in ~5 months — so by the same tiny-n logic that refuted leads #1/#2 it is not a bottleneck. Reclassify to refuted and remove the unsupported 'grows unbounded' growth-frontier framing.

diff_articulation.what_changed: N1 reclassified VERIFIED/#9 -> REFUTED with the dormancy evidence (43 records, 32-day creation window, ~5 months idle); added to the §2 refuted table; struck from the §0 summary table and the §5 one-hour fix list; removed the §4/§6 'art-jobs growth trajectory' production-data frontier row (unsupported hook). Notes that the store consolidation is proposed in the still-open PR #189 as correctness cleanup, not a perf fix.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: Documentation-only correction of a mis-ranked finding; aligns the audit's classification with its own tiny-n refutation logic and removes a manufactured growth narrative.

commands_run: grep review: no remaining reference treats N1/#9 as confirmed; §0/§2/§3/§4/§5/§6 internally consistent

files.intent: Reclassify finding N1 (art-jobs store) as refuted; remove the growth-frontier hook.

architecture_scan.acknowledgement_notes: Docs-only classification correction; no code or runtime change.
